step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the assessment presented above. One additional key factor supporting why HbS exhibits decreased mobility on gel electrophoresis compared to HbA involves the difference in charge distribution among subunits after the mutation. Valine introduces fewer negative charges per molecule because it carries no net charge unlike glutamic acid, which typically contributes negatively charged carboxyl groups. Consequently, this reduction in total surface area available for electrostatic attraction further hinders efficient movement through the porous medium of the gel, contributing to slower migration rates characteristic of HbS samples.
